entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 8 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 8 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-08 11:12AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Second Test, day one LIVE updates: Smith and Labuschagne tons put Australia on top
  • Man dies after crash involving truck, bus and several cars in Melbourne
  • As it happened: Former Japanese PM Shinzo Abe assassinated; Nick Kyrgios through to Wimbledon final; NSW ICAC witness found dead
  • International search underway for missing art
  • Warner continues Swans onslaught
  • Knights 'cult figure' produces astonishing minute of play
  • Perth soccer star opens up about the single punch that changed his life
  • Former Japanese prime minister Shinzo Abe has died after being shot during a speech in Nara.
  • Former Japanese PM Shinzo Abe dead
  • Pain without pay: Injured workers take on insurance giants
  • Star boss to front Qld casino law hearing before broader inquiry begins
  • Young Rabbitoh floored by 'vicious' head knock
  • Dismay after ‘wise friend’ to Australia, Shinzo Abe, assassinated
  • ‘He’s already made us the proudest family in the world’: Brother pays tribute to Kyrgios
  • Punter claims he was unfairly banned by Betfair for using ‘stream’
  • How Sydney Uni’s Guy Porter became Eddie’s England bolter
  • Ratten awarded with Saints extension
  • ‘Pretty sweet position’: History, luck and science on Kyrgios’ side after Nadal walkover
  • Finucane 'heartbroken' for injured McLean
  • Latrell to thrive on 'big honour': Johns
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • Albanese must back up new ministerial conduct code with actions
  • Best of cartoons, July 8, 2022
  • Citizenship, voting rights changes flagged for New Zealanders after Albanese-Ardern talks
  • For new government, consensus is the word
  • Australia must look east more than west for export fortunes
  • Herd Bali travellers through biosecurity to avoid agricultural catastrophe
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • Best of cartoons, July 7, 2022
  • Treasurer to measure ‘wellbeing’ pay-off from economy in first budget
  • Albanese to keep ‘bonk ban’ for ministers but stop them using blind trusts
  • Leisl Smith murder trial ends without a verdict after accused killer takes his own life
  • Loving two people a ‘national sport’ in France, Chris Dawson’s barrister says
  • A look back at a forgotten Sydney
  • NSW government commits to changes to mothballed train fleet if unions halt action
  • Second attempt to have Guy Sebastian’s ex-manager Titus Day detained
  • Residents return for flood clean-up as leaders look towards mitigation, prevention
  • ICAC witness found dead in office after giving evidence
  • Global executive was second woman preferred ahead of Barilaro for trade job
  • Stronger public health message needed in face of mask complacency

  • Former Japanese PM Shinzo Abe assassinated

  • Boris Johnson resigns as UK prime minister

  • Former Japanese prime minister Shinzo Abe shot while giving speech

  • Boris Johnson has quit, so who’s in for the Tory leadership?
  • Former Japanese PM shot during speech
  • Reporter captures moment Shinzo Abe was shot during speech
  • Former Japanese PM Shinzo Abe shot
  • Boris Johnson resigns as UK PM
  • Boris Johnson resigns as British Prime Minister
  • Chauvin admits ‘he knew’ kneeling on George Floyd’s neck ‘was wrong’
  • Police respond to incident in southern Sydney
  • Tour Down Under gears up for return
  • Sydney cop faces court over sexual assault charges
  • Girl faces court over alleged assault
  • New service to help tackle anti-social behaviour on Victorian public transport
  • You can now get a fourth jab if you’re 30 and healthy. So should you?
  • Queensland travellers charged thousands for domestic flights
  • Busiest day for Melbourne Airport since the pandemic
  • Australian PM and NZ PM forge pathway for citizenship deal progress
  • The Sydney Morning Herald, Photos of the Week, July 7, 2022
  • Man found dead day before verdict in 2012 murder
  • Psychologist denies trying to steal $2m from partner’s estate
  • Do e-scooters need more regulation?
  • ‘I’m done with crypto’: Voyager bankruptcy rocks true believers
  • Putin’s desperation on show as he slowly loses the financial battle
  • ‘Underperforming and expensive’: Magellan facing critical test after another $5b in outflows
  • The Wrap: ASX posts best week since March as resources recover
  • The era of the ‘Millennial lifestyle subsidy’ is ending
  • Investigation into Santos’ swinging platform still running one year later
  • Elon Musk’s deal to buy Twitter in ‘serious jeopardy’
  • The war engulfing Ben and Jerry’s ice cream
  • Elizabeth Holmes’ former partner found guilty in Theranos fraud trial
  • Twiggy-backed accelerator aims to give Indigenous entrepreneurs a dream start

  • Crown’s new faces know how to deal
  • Beach Energy backs carbon burial as Victoria lifts emissions goals
  • Swag on the Beat's TikTok comedy

  • Universal Music boss’ sudden exit sparks dramatic leadership change

  • Museums acquire viral Goose game so future generations can have a gander

  • This England official teaser trailer starring Kenneth Branagh
  • Financial woes, faked deaths, disguises: The real story of an Australian ‘charlatan’

  • 10 years of Nanoleaf
  • Apple’s ‘extreme’ iPhone security to combat state-sponsored hacks
  • Floodwaters recede as thousands return home to assess damage
  • ‘How many times do we have to go through this?’ How an atmospheric river hit Sydney
  • National weather forecast for Friday July 8
  • NSW weather eases but disaster not over
  • ‘The woman was trapped’: Flood rescues across NSW as state turns to clean-up
  • Climate still Pacific’s biggest security threat, retired leaders say
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 8 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Boris Johnson’s zombie government could still have some life in it
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Severodonetsk faces ‘humanitarian disaster’; U.N. warns of ‘hunger catastrophe’

  • Abbott tests feds by urging Texas troopers to return migrants to border
  • Willie Lee Morrow, pioneer of Black hair care and styling, dies at 82

  • As car sank, teen saved 3 girls and a police officer in river rescue
  • Christian activists push for total abortion ban in South Carolina
  • With little outcry, Chicago’s bloody weekend eclipsed Highland Park toll
  • Post Politics Now: Biden, Harris highlighting efforts to protect abortion rights
  • Post Politics Now: Manchin, other Senate Democrats reach agreement on key part of retooled economic package

  • People from elite backgrounds increasingly dominate academia, data shows
  • Casualties from war in Ukraine include millions of the world’s poor
  • How to reduce credit card debt amid recession fears
  • The machine behind the ‘God particle’ is on the hunt for dark matter
  • NASA issues rebuke to Russia for using space station as war propaganda
  • Elon Musk’s deal to buy Twitter is in peril
  • Private nature retreat in Lovettsville, Va., for sale for $3.5M
  • Mortgage rates decline for second week in a row
  • In Great Falls, a family estate rises with a team effort
  • Messy Politics Won’t Keep Goldman Sachs Out of Texas
  • Ukraine Shouldn’t Overplay Its Hand With Allies
  • There’s Good News for the Global Economy, If You Look
  • Shinzo Abe’s Assassination Will Scar Japan Forever
  • What’s Next for China Evergrande, Crushed by Debt
  • What the Falling Yen Means for Japan’s Trade Partners
  • How UK’s Tories Will Elect Leader to Replace Johnson
  • The Twisted Tale of Russia’s Sanctions-Driven Default
  • Many more babies will be born post-'Dobbs.' We need to help them and their moms.
  • Your questions about covid-19, answered by Dr. Leana Wen
  • A year after its president’s assassination, Haiti is in turmoil
  • Boris Johnson’s downfall gives Britain a chance for a fresh start
  • 3 ways to build a pro-democracy coalition
  • I’m a journalist who stopped reading the news. Is the problem me — or our product?
  • For a way forward on abortion, look to the Defense of Marriage Act

  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 8 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Shinzo Abe, Japan’s Longest-Serving Prime Minister, Dies at 67
  • Two loud sounds, a plume of smoke and then Abe collapsed.
  • Shinzo Abe faced steep opposition to his push for a more militaristic Japan.

  • At Europe’s Largest Port, Russia Sanctions Meet Their Toughest Test

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 8 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 8 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-08 11:00AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Arrest warrant issued for former Hong Kong opposition party vice-chairman after no-show over allegations of false claims about face masks
  • Video: Hong Kong after the handover: the highs and lows of 25 years under ‘one country, two systems’
  • Video: A look back at Hong Kong 25 years since the handover
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong proposes closed-loop border reopening with mainland China, under which residents quarantine locally before crossing over; 2,945 cases logged
  • Hong Kong airport’s third runway opens for take-off and landing drills amid increase in passenger traffic
  • Firefighters battle blaze at Hong Kong mansion owned by former screen goddess Brigitte Lin and billionaire husband
  • College representatives from Chinese University of Hong Kong in talks for new student union 9 months after disbandment of 50-year-old body
  • Hong Kong has halted its Covid flight suspension mechanism. Here’s what the shift means for travellers and airlines
  • New chief of Hong Kong’s anti-graft agency says decade of experience fighting corruption in prisons will serve him well
  • Home buyers snap up Sino Land’s Villa Garda flats in Lohas Park, enticed by 16 per cent discounts
  • EU Parliament condemns arrest of Cardinal Joseph Zen, 4 other Hong Kong democracy activists
  • Career Crossroads: Hitting a new career milestone with CityU MBA’s Experiential Learning
  • UN Human Rights Committee grills Hong Kong officials over use, ‘dramatic scope’ of national security law
  • Universities in Hong Kong facing ‘crisis’ as teaching staff head for exits, warns head of city’s largest self-financing institution
  • Hong Kong’s Executive Council should be dissolved or reformed as it is a relic from British rule, says former Legco president Jasper Tsang
  • Hong Kong’s new administration should speed up building new infrastructure to ensure success of Northern Metropolis plan: experts
  • China-Britain relations expected to face further challenges no matter who replaces Boris Johnson as prime minister
  • China to implement from September strict new cross-border data transfer rules, complicating operations of international firms
  • China-backed AIIB picks Abu Dhabi for first overseas office, more could follow
  • Air China responds to viral video of loose wing screw on Boeing 737-NG flight
  • Shinzo Abe: a political titan who shaped Japan’s policies on China, defence, economy
  • China premier stresses data security after alleged leak affecting 1 billion Chinese residents triggers concerns
  • Beijing delivers double warning to US on Taiwan Strait tensions
  • China’s Korean expats, students lament loss of jobs and opportunities as mass exodus comes into focus
  • Covid-19: Beijing’s vaccine mandate stirs worries among residents with foreign jabs
  • WeChat closes Bloomberg’s financial news account, making it latest victim of China’s online control
  • China population: ‘extremely low’ birth rate in Wuhu highlights national demographic crisis
  • Was this ancient creature the backbone of early vertebrates?
  • US and Nato are in no shape to treat China like a threat or competitor amid crumbling credibility
  • US charges American government official in Beijing scheme to harass Chinese dissidents
  • China starts work on first commercial space launch site, to be ready by 2024
  • China’s Tencent, miHoYo remain on top of their industry with world’s most lucrative mobile games, despite sluggish market
  • China, emerging markets suffering worst capital exodus in 7 years with US$4 billion net outflow in June
  • Tencent launches US$1.5 billion fund to give Chinese scientists freedom to reach new heights of innovation
  • China embarks on state-led search for ‘disruptive’ innovations as it seeks to take the lead on tech
  • Jack Ma tours Dutch university to learn sustainable agriculture as Alibaba founder keeps low profile
  • China puts Taiwan ‘reunification’ strategy at heart of national revival plans
  • US-China trade: cancel tariffs for Americans’ sake and don’t impose new measures, commerce ministry urges
  • Russian FM looks to China for greater cooperation under pressure from the West
  • Philippine Nobel laureate Ressa loses appeal of cyber libel conviction
  • Shinzo Abe shooting: UK finance minister Nadhim Zahawi deletes tweet saying Japan ex-PM is dead
  • Did South Korean police use ‘excessive force’ to subdue a Vietnamese man holding a knife?
  • Japan’s former prime minister Shinzo Abe dies after attack by gunman
  • As Philippines economy stutters, can Marcos keep promises on more jobs, cheaper food?
  • Ukraine war: Is Vladimir Putin using oil to ‘blackmail’ Japan for slapping sanctions on Russia?
  • Ukraine war: energy-hungry South Asia forced to walk a fine line between Russia, China and US
  • As G20 set to meet with Russia and China on its mind, Bali hopes for tourism boost
  • After UK PM Boris Johnson resigns, potential leaders prepare
  • Oscar-nominated ‘Godfather’ actor James Caan dies at 82
  • Why did T-rex have tiny arms? New ‘gargoyle’ dinosaur offers clues
  • Theranos ex-president Ramesh ‘Sunny’ Balwani found guilty of fraud
  • Vladimir Putin says Russia’s campaign in Ukraine ‘hasn’t even started in earnest’
  • Boris Johnson clinging on as PM so he can hold wedding bash at Chequers country retreat, UK media says
  • US basketball star Brittney Griner pleads guilty to drug charges in Russia
  • Ukraine must fight corruption too, says UN chief
  • Nuclear war would force Earth into ‘little ice age’ – study shows after Russia warning
  • UK PM Boris Johnson is quitting – who could replace him?
  • China Resources buys into HQ of struggling Hong Kong cosmetic retailer Bonjour Holdings via joint venture for US$114.7 million
  • Luxury brand Tom Ford hires Goldman Sachs to explore potential sale that could be valued in the billions of dollars
  • Why Asia’s gradual Covid-19 recovery means it has less to fear from rising inflation
  • How Hong Kong’s longest-running life insurer will help drive city’s recovery as it marks 125th anniversary
  • YouTube says its TikTok rival Shorts is helping musicians rapidly grow subscribers
  • Hong Kong stocks gain on hopes of strengthening recovery amid easing of travel restrictions and improved Chinese data
  • Elon Musk’s dispute with Twitter over bots puts deal at risk, but social media company intends to complete merger
  • Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission CEO Ashley Alder to leave post in January to join UK regulator as chairman
  • Top banker quits HSBC after suspension over climate-change comments
  • US start-up’s bacteria can convert gas emissions from Shougang’s Beijing steel mills into ethanol
  • Former HKMA chief Joseph Yam calls for more ‘capital mobility’ in Greater Bay Area
  • Fine dining or casual, French restaurants in Hong Kong are alive and well, as recent openings Auor and Terroirs by LQV show
  • Vitamin D from sunshine could help keep dementia at bay, a study shows, and fight other diseases
  • K-drama actor and Twenty-Five Twenty-One star Nam Joo-hyuk hit by more allegations of school bullying
  • Worn by celebrities like Zendaya and model Emily Ratajkowski, fashion designer Nensi Dojaka’s clothes explore interplay between sensuality and strength
  • Xiaomi 12s Ultra review: the best 2022 phone, its Leica camera makes Apple iPhone 13 Pro photos look flat, and battery life is unbeatable
  • Gourmet meals for under US$5: 2022 Michelin Guide Singapore adds 9 entries to its Bib Gourmand list of low-cost restaurants and food stalls
  • In HBO Go’s epic series Legacy, set in 1920s Shanghai, three stepsisters look to take over their wealthy family’s business
  • Steven Spielberg’s China-set Empire of the Sun – the first American film to be shot in Shanghai since the 1940s – forgot one important element: the Chinese
  • Netflix-topping ‘Money Heist: Korea’ K-drama remake explores reunification with North Korea, creator explains
  • How many children does Elon Musk have now? His 9 (and counting) kids revealed, from new twins with Shivon Zilis, and baby X Æ A-Xii, to trans daughter Vivian Wilson ... meet his brood and their mum
  • Inside Boris Johnson’s controversial lavish life as Britain’s prime minister, from his US$2 million net worth to dropping thousands on take-out, home refurbishments and that NYE trip
  • Selling Sunset’s Christine Quinn listed this US$34 million Miami penthouse through her new firm – take a look inside the ultra-luxury, 43rd floor home that you can even buy with cryptocurrency
  • How Fendi got East-West fusion right at Paris Fashion Week: Kim Jones’ autumn/winter 2022-23 haute couture collection made style stops in Kyoto, Paris and Rome – tastefully
  • 6 best new Hong Kong restaurants with spectacular sea views, from ADD+ and Mosu in West Kowloon’s M+, to Salisterra and Radical Chic, which have new chefs
  • Mukesh Ambani’s million-dollar motorcade: Asia’s second richest man owns the most expensive car in India, along with over 70 luxury rides including Bentleys and Rolls-Royces in his ‘Jio Garage’
  • Meet Hunter Schafer, set to star in the Hunger Games prequel: the trans icon rose to fame in Euphoria, is one of Zendaya’s BFFs, and recently modelled for Prada alongside Kendall Jenner
  • Meet Interceptor’s Elsa Pataky: she’s Chris Hemsworth’s wife, founded her own skincare line and has matching tattoos with Miley Cyrus – but why did she take over Natalie Portman’s kissing scenes in Thor?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 8 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: Guardian: 8 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Luhansk governor warns of ‘humanitarian disaster’; Moscow’s chief rabbi steps down over war – live

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
